slide0.title:
The new generation of 
renewable energy born from 
an innovator's dream
slide0.subtitle:
For years we pursued an impossible idea:
a continuous energy generator, without internal combustion, without emissions.
 We succeeded.

slide1.title: The renewable energy generator that never stops
slide1.subtitle:
APWEC is the Italian patented technology that generates energy by harnessing atmospheric pressure and controlled internal airflows.
Zero emissions. Operates approx. 8,660 hours per year, independent of sunlight and natural wind.

sixPhase._comment_researchProducts: Showcase section for products born from Sinersys research.
sixPhase._seo_note: Target keywords EN: 6-phase engine, Italian energy research, patented technology, renewable generator, thermodynamic innovation

researchProducts.sectionSubtitle: We continue to invest in advanced research on internal combustion engines to optimise every phase of the thermodynamic cycle, improve energy efficiency and reduce fuel waste.

product1.title: 6-Phase Engine
product1.subtitle: A new crankshaft system inside the engine that makes the isochoric function possible and overcomes the structural limitations of the connecting rod–crank pair, configuring an unprecedented thermodynamic cycle with six distinct high-efficiency phases.

slide2.title:
Industrial engineering and
 energy innovation
slide2.subtitle:
Sinersys's expertise in advanced mechanical design
 has found its greatest purpose in distributed renewable energy.
 A long journey of study and research,
 a milestone that changes everything.

slide3.title: Every APWEC is a concrete step towards energy independence.
slide3.subtitle:
Sinersys researches and develops scalable systems for continuous renewable energy generation,
designed for companies, public bodies, industrial operators and private users who want to reduce energy costs,
cut emissions and achieve energy self-sufficiency
in a measurable and verifiable way.

scatteredCards.label: Industrialisation
scatteredCards.title: Production
scatteredCards.subtitle: Bringing APWEC from its initial phase to large-scale industrial production. Certified, scalable machines ready for the global renewable energy market.

scatteredCards.label: Technology Development
scatteredCards.title: Expansion
scatteredCards.subtitle: Developing new APWEC power families — from 1 kW to 10 MW — to meet the energy needs of SMEs, large industries and public or private bodies in every geographic area.

scatteredCards.label: Global Impact
scatteredCards.title: Independence
scatteredCards.subtitle: Making energy self-sufficiency a concrete and measurable reality: zero emissions, zero grid dependence, verifiable savings from the first year of operation.

cta.title: Participate in the industrial development of APWEC
cta.subtitle: We are seeking industrial partners for the industrialisation, production and distribution of APWEC power families.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How does APWEC generate energy without wind?
faq.a1: APWEC harnesses atmospheric pressure and Earth's gravitational field. The pressure difference between the interior and exterior generates a continuous airflow that powers the system.
faq.a2: No. Operation depends on the difference in atmospheric pressure. When this falls below a minimum threshold, the system stops.
faq.a3: An initial fan generates the airflow. After start-up, it is powered by the energy produced by the system itself.
faq.a4: It operates for up to approximately 8,660 hours per year, with around 110 hours dedicated to maintenance.
faq.a5: Yes. The rotor is enclosed within a closed structure, reducing risks compared to traditional wind generators.

hero.title: An Italian dream
hero.subtitle:
We put all our passion and determination into pursuing dreams and achieving important milestones.
 The origins of Sinersys are rooted in decades of experience across various sectors of mechanics, thermodynamics and fluid dynamics. A long journey that led to the development of several objectives throughout our history.
hero.slide1title:
From the post-war era
to the energy of the future
hero.slide1subtitle: One hundred years of industrial collaborations, patents filed worldwide and an obsessive pursuit of fluid dynamics principles produced APWEC®
hero.mission: Sinersys was born as the evolution of a long entrepreneurial and engineering tradition developed across mechanical design, thermodynamic research and the study of fluid motion.

whoweare.title: Innovation built on decades of technical expertise and mechanical development
whoweare.p1:
Sinersys is a department of MUI, specialised in the study and development of advanced technologies, where the APWEC and 6-Phase Engine innovations have emerged in recent years.
 Motor Union Italia — active since the early 1900s in the automotive sector — carries with it a technical-scientific heritage built on the foundations of mechanical design and the energy transition.
whoweare.p2:
The expertise developed provided the foundations for tackling ambitious challenges, including the creation of a continuously operating energy generation system, from a renewable source, without dependence on environmental variables and without internal combustion.
 It also achieved the development of a revolutionary internal combustion engine with the isochoric function.

timeline.heading: Over a century of industrial innovation: the history that led to APWEC®
timeline.subheading: Each phase of Sinersys's history represents a real accumulation of expertise, patents and scientific and technical developments.

t1919.title: The industrial origins of the group
t1919.description: The roots of today's Sinersys go back to 1919, when the original entrepreneurial nucleus entered the first European mechanical and motor development programmes. It was the beginning of a long technical tradition that would accompany the group for over a century.

t1964.title: Entry into international automotive
t1964.description: Under the IAP brand, the group became the official importer of Honda products in Italy, beginning a strategic collaboration that would lead in 1971 to the construction of the Honda production plant in Val di Sangro. The industrial dimension took on an international scope.

t1965.title: Expansion into distribution of major European brands
t1965.description: Motauto was established, a structure dedicated to the exclusive importation into Italy of Volvo, Saab and Hanomag-Henschel. The company consolidated logistical, commercial and industrial expertise in the management of vehicles, components and mechanical technologies.

t1977.title: Foundation of the B-Art R&D centre
t1977.description: B-Art was established, a research and development centre. Numerous international industrial patents were born from this laboratory, including the first studies on the six-phase engine with isochoric function.

t1978.title: Start of studies on fluid motion and friction reduction
t1978.description: Structured research began on fluid dynamic motion within controlled geometries, initially applied to the reduction of aerodynamic friction in vehicles. These studies represent the theoretical foundation that, in subsequent years, led to the conception of the APWEC® system.

t1998.title: Sinersys born as an energy spin-off
t1998.description: After decades of experience in mechanical engineering and industrial design, the group launched Sinersys (Sinergia di Sistemi), a department entirely dedicated to the development of innovations.

t2002.title: Start of specific scientific studies on the APWEC® generator
t2002.description: Sinersys inaugurated the scientific programme dedicated to the creation of a 'green' generator, operating with fluid-air. The experimental phase began that would lead to the creation of the first functional prototype and the validation of the physical principle of the APWEC® generator.

t2008.title: Filing of the Eolic Silo® patent
t2008.description: After the first prototype tests and engineering verifications, Sinersys filed the Eolic Silo patent, the first technical step of the current APWEC, an energy conversion system based on induction chambers and controlled atmospheric pressure.

t2010.title: Filing of the APWEC® patent and international validation
t2010.description: The APWEC® (Atmospheric Pressure Wind Eolic Collector) patent was filed, an evolution of the Eolic Silo technology. The system underwent test reports conducted by TÜV Thüringen and the University of Ilmenau, computing centres and university departments, consolidating the scientific credibility of the project.

t2022.title: Start of the new CFD simulation programme

t2024.title: Further patent to optimise fluid-air intake

team.heading: The people who never stopped believing in the dream
team.subheading: Engineers, researchers and designers with decades of experience in industrial automotive, advanced mechanical engineering and the development of renewable energy technologies. Their determination to pursue an objective that many considered impossible with APWEC.

member0.name: Antonio Luca Biagini
member0.bio: Experience in automotive, cogeneration and industrial development. Responsible for APWEC development since 2012.

member1.name: Gianluca Acciarri
member1.bio: Specialised in fluid dynamics and design, he led the development of the APWEC prototype.

team.joinTitle: Want to be part of the team redefining renewable energy generation?
team.joinSubtitle: We are looking for engineers, researchers and professionals who share our vision: a world in which clean, continuous and accessible energy is the norm, not the exception.

slide0.title: OUR ROOTS
slide0.subtitle: Over a century of Italian engineering. A dream that changed the way energy is produced.

slide1.title: FROM AN IMPOSSIBLE DREAM TO THE GREATEST INNOVATION IN RENEWABLE ENERGY
slide1.subtitle: There is an idea that obsessed us for years: to build a generator capable of producing energy continuously, without consuming fuels, without depending on wind or sun. An idea that many considered unachievable. We pursued it, and we turned it into a patented reality. Sinersys was born from this determination: uniting over a century of Italian mechanical expertise with a radical vision of the energy transition.

slide2.title: FROM THE AUTOMOTIVE INDUSTRY TO THE CLEAN ENERGY REVOLUTION
slide2.subtitle: Our collaborations with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler taught us that true engineering accepts no compromises. That same precision — applied today to the development of renewable energy generation systems — is what makes APWEC a reliable, efficient technology ready for the global industrial market.

slide3.title: Innovating in the energy sector means taking responsibility for the future.
slide3.subtitle: Sinersys develops solutions for distributed renewable energy generation that allow companies, public bodies and industrial operators to concretely reduce consumption, cut CO₂ emissions and increase the energy resilience of their plants — with certified, scalable, high-performance technologies.

slide0.title: APWEC
slide0.subtitle:
Atmospheric Pressure Wind Energy Collector — the Italian renewable energy generator 
that uses fluid-air depression, producing electricity continuously.

slide1.title: Clean energy from the atmosphere
slide1.subtitle:
The system generates a controlled internal airflow
 that reaches high speeds and drives a rotor connected to the alternator.

slide2.title: Built for decades of continuous and reliable energy production
slide2.subtitle: Modular components designed to operate in closed or open environments, both civil and industrial. Certified reliability, low maintenance, ground-level access, maximum energy output.

slide3.title: Operational in less than 48 hours: renewable energy that integrates into your plant
slide3.subtitle: Ground-level installation, indoor or outdoor operation, on/off at will, no need for natural wind or complex/tall structures to capture wind at height.

static.label: What is APWEC
static.title: The Italian technology based on high-velocity fluid and atmospheric depression
static.p1: APWEC is a system that uses fluid-air to generate continuous electrical energy. The internal airflow is accelerated and controlled to drive a rotor connected to a generator. A technology born from a dream pursued for decades: to demonstrate that it is possible to produce energy uninterruptedly without depending on fossil sources, such as wind, sun, tides or others.
static.p2: Unlike traditional renewable energy generation systems, APWEC does not depend directly on solar irradiation or meteorological variables, and can operate practically without interruption throughout the year. Real energy savings, zero emissions, independence from the electrical grid.

f0.label: Continuous Operation
f0.title: Up to 8,660 annual hours
f0.description: Constant energy production independent of weather conditions.

f1.label: Scalability
f1.title: From 1 kW to approx. 10 MW
f1.description: APWEC's modular architecture allows the plant to be sized according to the user's real energy needs.

f2.label: Maintenance
f2.title: Simple and safe
f2.description: Ground-level access, no work at height.

cta.title: Request a technical assessment
cta.subtitle: Energy potential analysis, industrial feasibility and economic model available on request.

howItWorks.label: System architecture
howItWorks.title:
How APWEC® works:
three sections we have named vessels
howItWorks.intro:
The APWEC® generator is composed of three vessels solidly fixed to one another. The first generates airflow with openings for atmospheric air intake.
 In the second vessel there are a series of annular Induction Chambers (I.C.) appropriately designed to accelerate the flow within this second section. The third converts the considerably accelerated high-speed airflow kinetically into electrical energy via a rotor connected to the alternator.

shell1.label: First Section
shell1.title: Flow generation and amplification
shell1.desc: A high-speed trigger jet enters the first annular Induction Chamber. The pressure difference generated according to Bernoulli's Principle draws atmospheric air from outside, progressively amplifying the airflow at each successive I.C. The casing material can be steel, aluminium or reinforced plastic for maximum construction flexibility and cost reduction.

shell2.label: Second Section
shell2.title: Conversion into electrical energy
shell2.desc2: The final flow, energised by the atmosphere, impacts the rotor with aerofoil-profile blades — the same aerodynamic principle as state-of-the-art wind turbines, with a decisive advantage: the flow is unidirectional, so the blades do not require a variable pitch mechanism. The integrated alternator produces current stabilised in voltage and frequency via inverter.
shell2.desc: Since the system operates in a duct and the flow is always unidirectional with the same airflow speed intensity, the APWEC rotor does not need a variable-pitch blade system, since the airflow speed always remains the same.

cdi0.label: I.C. 1 — Trigger
cdi0.desc: The trigger jet generates internal depression (Δp = ½ρv²). The force F = Δp × Area sets the air mass in motion with acceleration a = F/m.

cdi1.label: I.C. 2 — First amplification
cdi1.desc: The flow exiting the first chamber joins the trigger in the next chamber. The velocity grows according to V = √(2 × a × s), where s is the path in the I.C.

cdi2.label: I.C. n — Progressive amplification
cdi2.desc: Each chamber receives an increasingly energetic combined flow. The contribution of the external atmosphere increases at each stage, multiplying the flow rate without fuel consumption.

cdi3.label: Final I.C. — Output towards the rotor
cdi3.desc: The last induction chamber opens directly into the Second Section. The flow reaches the designed operating speed and impacts the rotor blades.

rotor.label: Rotor + Alternator
rotor.desc: Fixed aerofoil-profile blades (no variable pitch). Unidirectional flow. Stabilised current production via inverter.

physics.label: Physical principles underlying APWEC®
physics.title: The energy of the atmosphere transformed into electrical current
physics.intro: The operation of APWEC® is based on the synergy of established aerodynamic and fluid dynamic principles: Bernoulli's Principle for depression generation, Newton's Second Law for air mass acceleration, and the Betz and Kutta-Joukowski theorems for the conversion of kinetic energy into torque on the rotor.

formula0.desc: Bernoulli's Principle: internal depression draws in external air

formula1.desc: The force that accelerates the air mass in the induction chamber

formula2.desc: Velocity reached by the flow at the exit of each I.C.

p0.label: Bernoulli's Principle
p0.desc: The pressure difference between the external atmosphere and the internal depression of the Induction Chamber is the main driver of APWEC®. No fuel, no thermal source: only the pressure of the air that surrounds us.

p1.label: Newton's Second Law
p1.desc: The force generated by the depression acts on the air mass contained in the I.C., imparting a measurable acceleration to it. The flow exits the chamber at a speed well above the initial trigger.

p2.label: Betz Theorem
p2.desc: The APWEC® rotor operates according to the same principles of conversion of wind kinetic energy into mechanical energy used by large wind turbines. The difference is the source of the flow: not meteorological wind, but a controlled and continuous internal flow.

p3.label: Kutta-Joukowski Theorem
p3.desc: The aerofoil profile of the blades generates lift on the incident flow, transmitting torque to the generator shaft. The unidirectional flow eliminates the need for a variable pitch mechanism, simplifying the mechanical structure and reducing maintenance costs.

advantages.label: APWEC® vs. traditional wind turbines
advantages.title: Technical and economic advantages over the state of the art
advantages.intro:
The basic concept is Simplicity. Simplicity of calculation, construction and operation.
The comparison between APWEC® and large conventional wind turbines highlights structural advantages that impact construction, maintenance, installation and operating costs. APWEC, a next-generation generator designed according to the automotive construction philosophy.

a0.label: Operability
a0.title: Operation up to 8,660 annual hours
a0.desc: Continuous energy production independent of weather conditions. No dependence on wind or solar irradiation: the system operates 24/7 for the entire solar year, with scheduled stoppages for maintenance only.

a1.label: Maintenance safety
a1.title: Ground-level maintenance, no work at height
a1.desc: All service operations take place on the ground. No intervention at significant heights, eliminating the operational risks typical of conventional wind turbines that require specialised technicians working at height.

a2.label: Construction simplicity
a2.title: Fewer components, automotive-style construction
a2.desc: APWEC® requires fewer parts compared to the state of the art. The automotive-derived construction philosophy guarantees standardisation, global availability of components and reduced production costs in large series.

a3.label: Fixed-pitch blades
a3.title: No variable pitch mechanism
a3.desc: The internal airflow is unidirectional and controlled. The rotor blades operate at fixed pitch, eliminating the variable pitch control system: one of the main sources of failure and cost in large wind turbines.

a4.label: Installation flexibility
a4.title: Indoor and outdoor operation, adjacent to the user
a4.desc: APWEC® can be installed inside industrial buildings or outdoors, without the need for towers or elevated structures. Installation adjacent to the end user reduces transmission losses and grid connection costs.

a5.label: Analogy with industrial turbines
a5.title: Structure derived from the industrial gas turbine
a5.desc: The physical configuration of APWEC® follows the structure of industrial gas turbines, themselves derived from jet aircraft turbines. A mature, reliable technology with decades of engineering know-how behind it.

advantages.vsDesc:
Large wind turbines exclusively exploit the dynamic pressure of external wind and require minimum wind speed, tall towers, variable pitch mechanisms and maintenance at height.
APWEC® operates with a controlled internal flow, at ground level, regardless of external atmospheric conditions — a technically distinct and complementary category in the landscape of distributed renewable energy generation.

slide0.title: 6-Phase Engine
slide0.subtitle: The Italian constant-volume combustion engine with rotating connecting rod and semi-floating piston: more power, lower consumption, lower emissions.

slide1.title: The thermodynamic cycle reinvented
slide1.subtitle: A predetermined-profile eccentric replaces the classic connecting rod–crank system, eliminating dead centres and allowing perfect isochoric combustion at TDC.

slide2.title: Designed to maximise power and reduce passive losses
slide2.subtitle: Volumetric efficiency up to 120% of displacement, zero ignition advance and modular architecture derived from the automotive construction philosophy.

slide3.title: From automotive powertrain to industrial Stirling cycle
slide3.subtitle: The 6-phase crankshaft principle applies to internal combustion, hybrid and Stirling cycle engines, opening scenarios of near-zero pollution and further reduced consumption.

static.label: What is the 6-Phase Engine
static.title: The constant-volume combustion that redefines the modern engine
static.p1: The Motor Union Italia 6-Phase Engine is an internal combustion engine that realises a thermodynamic cycle in six phases over two revolutions of the crankshaft. The heart of the invention is the new crankshaft system: a predetermined-profile eccentric, integral with the crankshaft, on which a roller keyed to the piston pin slides. This eliminates the classic dead centres and allows the kinematic law of the piston to be precisely defined.
static.p2: The result is an Isochoric (Constant Volume) Combustion at Top Dead Centre with zero ignition advance, a prolonged intake at BDC with volumetric efficiencies greater than unity, and the elimination of losses due to combustion pressure during the advance period. All in a more compact, lighter engine built with an automotive philosophy.

f0.label: Thermal Efficiency
f0.title: Isochoric combustion at TDC
f0.description: Zero ignition advance: all the energy of the mixture is converted into useful work without drawing energy from the flywheel during compression.

f1.label: Volumetric Efficiency
f1.title: Up to 120% of displacement
f1.description: The prolonged intake with the piston stationary at BDC exploits the inertial velocity of the incoming mixture, achieving volumetric efficiencies greater than unity at medium and high speeds.

f2.label: Compactness
f2.title: Reduced powertrain volume and weight
f2.description: Integrated with the belt-free, pulley-free stepless automatic transmission, the powertrain significantly reduces bulk, weight and costs compared to current solutions.

cta.title: Request a technical assessment
cta.subtitle: Application analysis, construction feasibility and economic model available on request.

howItWorks.label: The 6-phase cycle
howItWorks.title:
How the 6-Phase Engine works:
the new eccentric crankshaft system
howItWorks.intro: The system is based on a predetermined-profile eccentric integral with the crankshaft. A roller, keyed to the piston pin, slides on the eccentric profile: the piston therefore follows the kinematic law impressed in the profile, not the sinusoidal one of the classic connecting rod–crank mechanism. The designer can thus define dwells at TDC for isochoric combustion and dwells at BDC to maximise filling.

shell1.label: First Principle — Isochoric Combustion
shell1.title: Zero ignition advance, maximum energy
shell1.desc: In the traditional system, ignition advance is necessary to complete combustion before the piston moves away from TDC, but it draws energy from the flywheel during compression. With the predetermined-profile eccentric, the piston stops at TDC for the chosen angle: combustion occurs at constant volume, with zero advance, transferring all thermal energy into pressure available for the expansion phase.

shell2.label: Second Principle — Prolonged Intake
shell2.title: Filling greater than unity at medium and high speeds
shell2.desc: At Bottom Dead Centre the piston stops for a programmed angular interval, exploiting the inertial velocity of the incoming mixture column. The volumetric efficiency exceeds unity at medium and high speeds — up to 120% of geometric displacement — without resorting to forced supercharging, increasing mean effective pressure (MEP) naturally and reliably.

advantages.label: 6-Phase Engine vs. conventional engines
advantages.title: Technical and economic advantages over the state of the art
advantages.intro: The comparison between the Motor Union Italia 6-Phase Engine and conventional 4-stroke engines highlights structural advantages in efficiency, emissions, compactness and costs. A technology designed for the era of the energy transition.

a0.label: Thermodynamic efficiency
a0.title: Isochoric combustion: no advance losses
a0.desc: Eliminating ignition advance removes energy losses during compression. All the pressure generated by combustion is available in the expansion phase, increasing specific power for the same displacement.

a1.label: Natural filling
a1.title: Volumetric efficiency up to 120%
a1.desc: The dwell at BDC exploits the inertia of the inducted mixture: at medium and high speeds, filling exceeds 100% of geometric displacement, increasing MEP without mechanical supercharging.

a2.label: Construction simplicity
a2.title: Fewer components, automotive philosophy
a2.desc: The eccentric system replaces the complex connecting rod–crank mechanism with a precision-machined profile. The automotive construction philosophy guarantees standardisation, reduced production costs and global availability of components.

a3.label: Fixed-pitch blades — rotor
a3.title: Rotating connecting rod and semi-floating piston
a3.desc: The rotating connecting rod and semi-floating piston configuration promotes thermal stability at high temperatures, optimal lubrication and a piston mass equal to or less than that of an equivalent conventional displacement, reducing inertial forces.

a4.label: Emissions and acoustics
a4.title: Towards near-zero pollution
a4.desc: Integrated with the Stirling cycle, the engine targets emissions compliant with California standards (among the most stringent in the world), with acoustic pollution below 70 dB and use of very low octane fuels, free of anti-knock additives.

a5.label: Compact powertrain
a5.title: Reduced volume and weight with stepless transmission
a5.desc: Combined with the belt-free, pulley-free stepless automatic transmission, the powertrain significantly reduces bulk and weight compared to current solutions, with direct benefits for consumption, vehicle dynamics and integration costs.

advantages.vsDesc:
Conventional 4-stroke engines require ignition advance, operate with volumetric efficiencies below unity and accumulate passive losses in the compression phase.
The Motor Union Italia 6-Phase Engine eliminates these structural losses through isochoric combustion, prolonged intake and the new crankshaft system — a technically distinct category that positions itself as the natural evolution of the internal combustion engine for the energy transition.

research.label: Research and Development
research.title: The Research Centre that transformed an impossible dream into a patent
research.subtitle: For decades we pursued an idea that the world considered unattainable: to build a generator capable of producing energy continuously, without consuming fuels and without depending on sun or wind. Today that dream is a patented reality.
research.p1: The Sinersys Research and Development Centre is staffed by engineers and designers of proven competence in the energy sector, with international experience in applied research. Their goal has always been one: to create a system capable of producing renewable energy continuously, without the need to consume energy from outside.
research.p2: Sinersys was born within Motor Union Italia, active since 1919 in the industrial automotive sector and motor engineering. The expertise accumulated over more than a century of advanced mechanical design — also developed through collaborations with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler — constituted the technical-scientific heritage on which the APWEC technology for clean and continuous energy generation was developed.
research.p3: Motor Union Italia created Sinersys to respond to the growing needs for energy autonomy, reduced energy costs and reduced environmental impact from industries, public bodies and operators in the energy sector.

innovation.label: Patented Innovation
innovation.title: Beyond the state of the art in continuous renewable energy generation
innovation.p1: The technology underlying APWEC is based on the synergistic action of aerodynamic and fluid dynamic principles — rectilinear motion, vortex and source/sink — described by linear differential equations (Laplace equations). This rigorous mathematical foundation guarantees predictability, reproducibility and engineering robustness to the system.
innovation.p2: Compared to traditional wind generators, which exclusively exploit the dynamic pressure of wind (½ρV²), APWEC operates according to different and complementary principles, achieving higher specific power outputs and producing energy continuously regardless of atmospheric conditions.

comparison.label: Technology Comparison
comparison.title: Sinersys Eolic Silo vs. traditional generators: concrete advantages

costibenefici.title: Costs and benefits in energy savings
costibenefici.body: The comparison between the Sinersys Eolic Silo and traditional wind generation systems shows clear net advantages in terms of cost per kWh produced: the 200 kW rotor has a maximum diameter of 3.5 metres compared to 30 metres for an equivalent WEC, with estimated production costs between €120,000 and €150,000 per unit.

silenziosita.title: Silence and reduced acoustic impact
silenziosita.body: The APWEC rotor, operating inside an insulated casing, guarantees drastically lower acoustic emissions compared to the large blade rotors of traditional WECs. A decisive advantage for installations in urban contexts or near populated areas.

manutenzione.title: Ground-level maintenance: safer, less costly
manutenzione.body: All APWEC maintenance operations are performed at ground level: generator and control equipment are accessible without the need for work at height. Less downtime means more hours of renewable energy production every year.

impatto.title: Minimised visual and environmental impact
impatto.body: For equal installed power, APWEC has a maximum height of approximately 20 metres — less than a quarter of an equivalent traditional WEC. The visual impact is significantly lower, with faster permitting processes and greater compatibility with local regulations.

certifications.label: Certifications and Quality
certifications.title: Certified quality, guaranteed energy efficiency over time
certifications.subtitle: Every Sinersys renewable energy generation system is designed, tested and certified according to international quality standards. The promised energy performance is the certified performance.

fullEnergyProject.title: FULL ENERGY PROJECT
fullEnergyProject.body: This is the exclusive certification attestation issued upon completion of the entire Sinersys process. It includes preliminary scientific studies, in-depth energy diagnostics and custom design of the renewable generation plant. Full Energy Project certifies the performance of the installed system and provides the client with verifiable documentation of expected yield and return on investment.

techService.title: TECH SERVICE
techService.body: Sinersys's continuous assistance, maintenance and optimisation programme. For every renewable energy plant designed and installed, Tech Service guarantees maximum energy performance over time through scheduled interventions, remote monitoring and periodic technical updates.

frontierEnergetiche.label: Energy Solutions
frontierEnergetiche.title: The new frontiers of renewable energy and energy efficiency
frontierEnergetiche.p1: Sinersys designs and builds high-efficiency cogeneration projects and plants and develops advanced technologies for the distributed generation of continuous renewable energy. From our century-long history in motor engineering comes a precise mission: to make energy independence accessible to industries, public bodies and commercial operators who want to concretely reduce energy costs, cut CO₂ emissions and increase the energy resilience of their plants.
frontierEnergetiche.p2: Sinersys provides efficient, flexible and scalable renewable energy generation systems that enable energy self-production with zero environmental impact. Every plant is custom-designed, certified with the Full Energy Project programme and supported over time by the Tech Service.

ethics.label: Code of Ethics
ethics.title: Code of Ethics
ethics.subtitle: The principles that guide every choice we make, every day.

s1.heading: Preamble and scope of application
s1.p1: Sinersys is a department of MUI, specialised in the study and development of advanced technologies, where the APWEC and 6-Phase Engine innovations have emerged in recent years. We operate with the conviction that innovation and responsibility are not contradictory values, but complementary ones: the quality of the work we do also depends on the quality of the relationships we build and the behaviours we adopt.
s1.p2: This Code of Ethics applies to all those who operate for or with Sinersys: employees, collaborators, consultants, suppliers and commercial partners. It is not a formal document that is an end in itself, but the written reflection of a corporate culture that prioritises substance over form.

s2.heading: Our values
s2.p1: Every decision we make — strategic, operational, commercial — is guided by the following fundamental principles:

v1.title: Integrity
v1.body: We maintain consistency between what we say and what we do. We take responsibility for our mistakes and do not minimise them.

v2.title: Conscious innovation
v2.body: We develop technology with full awareness of its social and environmental implications. Innovation that does not bring concrete benefits to the energy transition is not progress.

v3.title: Mutual respect
v3.body: Every person who interacts with Sinersys deserves respect for their dignity, regardless of role or position.

v4.title: Real sustainability
v4.body: We do not use sustainability as a marketing tool. Our environmental and social commitments are measurable and verifiable.

v5.title: Transparency
v5.body: We communicate clearly, even when the news is difficult. Trust is built with honesty, not with reassurances.

s3.heading: Work environment
s3.p1: Sinersys is a place where people must be able to work well. This requires active commitment from everyone:
s3.l1: Ensuring safe physical and digital environments, free from avoidable health and wellbeing risks.
s3.l2: Building a culture where disagreement is expressed openly and respectfully, without fear of retaliation.
s3.l3: Recognising and valuing the contribution of each person, regardless of gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation or personal convictions.
s3.l4: Investing in professional development as a mutual commitment, not a one-sided benefit.

s4.heading: Commercial relations
s4.p1: With clients and partners we build relationships that create long-term value. This means being honest about the limitations of our solutions, honouring contractual commitments even when it is costly to do so, and refusing commissions that would require compromising our integrity.
s4.p2: In the selection of suppliers we favour those who share comparable ethical and environmental standards to our own. We do not accept the principle that the lowest price justifies any compromise on working conditions or environmental impact in the supply chain.

s5.heading: Conflicts of interest and gifts
s5.p1: A conflict of interest arises when a personal interest could interfere with decisions made on behalf of Sinersys. We ask everyone to:
s5.l1: Proactively report any situation that may generate a real or apparent conflict of interest.
s5.l2: Refrain from making decisions in conflict situations, delegating them to a third party.
s5.l3: Not accept or offer gifts, hospitality or other benefits that go beyond normal professional courtesy practices (indicative limit: €50 per single episode).
s5.l4: Not use confidential information obtained in the exercise of their role for personal purposes or on behalf of third parties.

s6.heading: Confidentiality and data protection
s6.p1: Confidential information is an asset that must be protected. Every collaborator is required to treat it with the same care with which they would want their own personal data to be treated by others.
s6.p2: The processing of personal data is carried out in compliance with EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR) and current Italian legislation.

s7.heading: Anti-corruption and regulatory compliance
s7.p1: Sinersys operates in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations in every country in which it is present. Corruption — in any form — is incompatible with our values and our business model.
s7.l1: It is prohibited to make payments or promises of benefits to public or private officials to obtain undue advantages.
s7.l2: Accounting must faithfully reflect every transaction. There are no slush funds, unregistered payments or false records.
s7.l3: Anyone who detects or suspects a violation of this Code can report it confidentially through official channels, with guaranteed anonymity and protection from retaliation.

s8.heading: Violations and consequences
s8.p1: Proven violations of this Code of Ethics entail consequences proportionate to the severity of the conduct, up to and including termination of the employment or collaboration relationship. For reports: etica@sinersys.it

privacy.label: Privacy Policy
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect your personal data.

s1.heading: Data Controller
s1.p1: This information notice is drafted pursuant to Art. 13 of EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR) and applies to anyone who visits the sinersys.it website or interacts with Sinersys's services.
s1.addressVal: Zona Industriale C.da Saletti - 66041 Piazzano di Atessa (Ch.)

s2.heading: Categories of data processed
s2.p1: Depending on the type of interaction with our services, we process the following categories of personal data:

d1.title: Navigation data
d1.body: IP address, browser type and operating system, pages visited, access timestamps.

d2.title: Voluntarily communicated data
d2.body: First name, last name, email address, telephone number and any other information entered in contact forms.

d3.title: Contractual data
d3.body: Information necessary for the conclusion and management of supply contracts.

d4.title: Technical usage data
d4.body: Aggregate statistics on the use of site features, collected in anonymised form.

s3.heading: Purposes and legal bases
s3.p1: Personal data is processed for specific purposes, each with its own legal basis:
s3.l1: Response to contact requests and management of pre-contractual relationships (Art. 6.1.b GDPR).
s3.l2: Fulfilment of contractual obligations relating to the supply of products and services (Art. 6.1.b GDPR).
s3.l3: Fulfilment of legal, tax and accounting obligations (Art. 6.1.c GDPR).
s3.l4: Sending of promotional communications and newsletters, exclusively with prior explicit consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR).
s3.l5: Statistical analysis of site usage (Art. 6.1.f GDPR, with right to object).

s4.heading: Recipients of data
s4.p1: Personal data is not sold to third parties. It may be communicated exclusively to:

b1.title: Data processors
b1.body: Technical service providers (hosting, email, CRM, analytics) who process data on our behalf based on specific contractual agreements.

b2.title: Professionals and consultants
b2.body: Accountants, legal consultants and other professionals who need access to certain data to carry out their assignment.

b3.title: Public authorities
b3.body: Law enforcement agencies, judicial or administrative authorities, exclusively upon formal request or in fulfilment of a legal obligation.

s5.heading: Extra-EU transfers
s5.p1: Some technical services we use may involve data transfers to non-EU countries. In such cases we apply the safeguards provided for by the GDPR: Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission.
s5.p2: The updated list of sub-processors and safeguards adopted is available upon request by writing to privacy@sinersys.it.

s6.heading: Your rights
s6.p1: As a data subject, you have the right to:

r1.title: Access (Art. 15)
r1.body: Obtain confirmation that we are processing your personal data and receive a copy of it.

r2.title: Rectification (Art. 16)
r2.body: Request the correction of inaccurate data or the completion of incomplete data.

r3.title: Erasure (Art. 17)
r3.body: Request erasure in the cases provided for by the regulations.

r4.title: Restriction (Art. 18)
r4.body: Request that processing be restricted to certain operations in specific circumstances.

r5.title: Portability (Art. 20)
r5.body: Receive the data provided in a structured, machine-readable format.

s6.contact: To exercise your rights write to privacy@sinersys.it. We will respond within 30 days.

s7.heading: Retention periods
s7.p1: We retain personal data for the strictly necessary time: contractual and tax data for 10 years; navigation data for a maximum of 13 months; data for marketing activities until consent is withdrawn or for a maximum of 24 months from the last interaction.
s7.p2: Upon expiry of the retention period, data is securely deleted or rendered permanently anonymous.

s8.heading: Updates
s8.p1: This information notice may be updated at any time. Substantial changes are communicated via notice on the site or by email to registered users.

cookies.label: Cookie Policy
cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: How we use cookies and tracking technologies on sinersys.it.

s1.heading: What are cookies
s1.p1: Cookies are small text files that websites deposit on the user's device during browsing. They allow the site to remember the user's actions and preferences over time.
s1.p2: In addition to traditional cookies, we use analogous technologies such as tracking pixels and local storage. This Cookie Policy applies to all these technologies.

s2.heading: Types of cookies we use
s2.p1: We classify the cookies we use into four categories:

c1.title: Essential technical cookies
c1.body: Necessary for the functioning of the site. They do not require consent and cannot be disabled.

c2.title: Analytical cookies
c2.body: Collect aggregated information about how users use the site. Data is anonymised before processing. Consent required.

c3.title: Functional cookies
c3.body: Allow the site to remember choices made by the user to offer a more personalised experience. Consent required.

c4.title: Marketing and profiling cookies
c4.body: Used to show relevant advertising content and measure campaign effectiveness. Explicit consent required.

s3.heading: Third-party cookies
s3.p1: We integrate the following third-party services into the site, each of which may install its own cookies:
s3.ga: Google Analytics: Google LLC's web analytics platform. Collects anonymised navigation data for statistical analysis.
s3.gtm: Google Tag Manager: service for the centralised management of tracking tags.
s3.p2: For complete information on the cookies installed by each service, consult the privacy policies of the individual providers.

s4.heading: How to manage cookies
s4.p1: You have full control over non-essential cookies. You can manage your preferences through:
s4.l1: The consent management panel, accessible via the 'Cookie settings' link in the footer.
s4.l2: Browser settings: Chrome, Firefox, Safari and Edge allow you to view, delete and block cookies.
s4.l3: Provider opt-out tools: Google Analytics Opt-out (tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout).
s4.l4: Management platforms for behavioural advertising: YourOnlineChoices (youronlinechoices.eu).

s5.heading: Impact of disabling
s5.p1: Disabling essential technical cookies is not possible without seriously impairing the functioning of the site. Disabling analytical and marketing cookies does not affect your ability to browse.
s5.p2: If you use your browser in private/incognito mode, cookies are automatically deleted when the window is closed.

s6.heading: Updates and contacts
s6.p1: This Cookie Policy is updated periodically. We recommend visiting this page regularly.
s6.p2: For any questions relating to the use of cookies on sinersys.it, write to us at privacy@sinersys.it.
